info.title: AgentQueue API
info.description: REST API for the agent-queue daemon.

post.summary: Api Execute
post.description: Run a CommandHandler command (backward-compat envelope).

get.description: Return all tool definitions for CLI auto-generation.

Full system health status.

Returns 200 when all checks pass, 503 when any check indicates
a problem (ok: False).

Readiness probe — checks messaging platform and database.

Returns 200 when ready, 503 when not.
Serve plan content as an HTML page.

post.summary: Validate an agent profile's install dependencies. Checks that required commands, npm packages, and pip packages are available.
post.description: Validate an agent profile's install dependencies. Checks that required commands, npm packages, and pip packages are available.

post.summary: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use add_workspace to add agent capacity to a project.
post.description: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use add_workspace to add agent capacity to a project.

post.summary: Create a new agent profile. Profiles configure agents with specific tools, MCP servers, model overrides, and system prompt additions. Assign profiles to tasks (profile_id) or set as project defaults (default_profile_id).
post.description: Create a new agent profile. Profiles configure agents with specific tools, MCP servers, model overrides, and system prompt additions. Assign profiles to tasks (profile_id) or set as project defaults (default_profile_id).

post.summary: Create a project-scoped agent profile.  Composes ``project:<project_id>:<agent_type>`` as the profile id and writes the vault markdown.  When ``seed_from_global`` is true (default), starts from the matching global ``<agent_type>`` profile so the override is a delta.
post.description: Create a project-scoped agent profile.  Composes ``project:<project_id>:<agent_type>`` as the profile id and writes the vault markdown.  When ``seed_from_global`` is true (default), starts from the matching global ``<agent_type>`` profile so the override is a delta.

post.summary: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use remove_workspace instead.
post.description: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use remove_workspace instead.

post.summary: Delete an agent profile. Any tasks or projects referencing it will have their profile cleared.
post.description: Delete an agent profile. Any tasks or projects referencing it will have their profile cleared.

post.summary: Remove a project-scoped agent profile (vault file + DB row).
post.description: Remove a project-scoped agent profile (vault file + DB row).

post.summary: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use edit_project or workspace commands instead.
post.description: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces. Use edit_project or workspace commands instead.

post.summary: Edit an existing agent profile's properties.
post.description: Edit an existing agent profile's properties.

post.summary: Edit fields on a project-scoped agent profile.
post.description: Edit fields on a project-scoped agent profile.

post.summary: Export an agent profile as YAML. Optionally create a public GitHub gist.
post.description: Export an agent profile as YAML. Optionally create a public GitHub gist.

post.summary: Get the last error recorded for a task, including error classification, suggested fix, and agent summary.
post.description: Get the last error recorded for a task, including error classification, suggested fix, and agent summary.

post.summary: Get details of a specific agent profile.
post.description: Get details of a specific agent profile.

post.summary: Import an agent profile from YAML text or a GitHub gist URL. If the profile has an install manifest, dependencies are auto-installed.
post.description: Import an agent profile from YAML text or a GitHub gist URL. If the profile has an install manifest, dependencies are auto-installed.

post.summary: Install missing npm/pip dependencies for a profile's install manifest. System commands that are missing are reported for manual installation.
post.description: Install missing npm/pip dependencies for a profile's install manifest. System commands that are missing are reported for manual installation.

post.summary: List agent slots for a project. Each workspace is an agent slot: locked workspaces are 'busy', unlocked are 'idle'. Requires project_id (or an active project).
post.description: List agent slots for a project. Each workspace is an agent slot: locked workspaces are 'busy', unlocked are 'idle'. Requires project_id (or an active project).

post.summary: Discover available Claude Code tools and well-known MCP servers for use in agent profiles.
post.description: Discover available Claude Code tools and well-known MCP servers for use in agent profiles.

post.summary: List all agent profiles. Profiles are capability bundles that configure agents with specific tools, MCP servers, and system prompts.
post.description: List all agent profiles. Profiles are capability bundles that configure agents with specific tools, MCP servers, and system prompts.

post.summary: List per-agent-type profile rows for a project, including the global, scoped, and effective views — plus the project-scoped MCP tool catalog snapshot in the same response.
post.description: List per-agent-type profile rows for a project, including the global, scoped, and effective views — plus the project-scoped MCP tool catalog snapshot in the same response.

post.summary: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces.
post.description: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces.

post.summary: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces.
post.description: Deprecated — agents are now derived from workspaces.

post.summary: Run the orchestrator's profile resolution cascade for a (project_id, agent_type) pair and return the merged profile the next task launch would use.  Debug helper.
post.description: Run the orchestrator's profile resolution cascade for a (project_id, agent_type) pair and return the merged profile the next task launch would use.  Debug helper.

post.summary: Count files in a subdirectory of a project's system memory vault (``{data_dir}/vault/projects/<project_id>/memory/<path>``). Optionally filter by modification time via ``newer_than`` (ISO 8601 timestamp). Returns ``{count, total, missing?}``; a missing directory is reported as ``count: 0, missing: true`` rather than an error so the memory-consolidation playbook can handle first-run projects cleanly.
post.description: Count files in a subdirectory of a project's system memory vault (``{data_dir}/vault/projects/<project_id>/memory/<path>``). Optionally filter by modification time via ``newer_than`` (ISO 8601 timestamp). Returns ``{count, total, missing?}``; a missing directory is reported as ``count: 0, missing: true`` rather than an error so the memory-consolidation playbook can handle first-run projects cleanly.

post.summary: Perform targeted string replacement in a file. Finds old_string and replaces it with new_string. The old_string must be unique in the file (include surrounding context to disambiguate). Use replace_all=true to replace every occurrence.
post.description: Perform targeted string replacement in a file. Finds old_string and replaces it with new_string. The old_string must be unique in the file (include surrounding context to disambiguate). Use replace_all=true to replace every occurrence.

post.summary: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.py', 'src/**/*.ts'). Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time.
post.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.py', 'src/**/*.ts'). Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time.

post.summary: Search file contents using regex patterns (ripgrep-style). Supports context lines, case-insensitive search, file type filtering, and multiple output modes.
post.description: Search file contents using regex patterns (ripgrep-style). Supports context lines, case-insensitive search, file type filtering, and multiple output modes.

post.summary: List files and directories at a given path within a project workspace.
post.description: List files and directories at a given path within a project workspace.

post.summary: Read a file's contents. Path can be absolute or relative to the workspaces root. Supports offset/limit for reading specific portions of large files.
post.description: Read a file's contents. Path can be absolute or relative to the workspaces root. Supports offset/limit for reading specific portions of large files.

post.summary: Read a markdown file from a project's system memory vault. Resolves paths under ``{data_dir}/vault/projects/<project_id>/memory/<path>``, which lives outside the regular workspace sandbox. Use this for reading consolidation markers, insight files, or knowledge entries from the agent-queue vault. Path traversal outside the project's memory directory is rejected. Returns ``{missing: true, error}`` when the file does not exist so the memory-consolidation playbook can treat it as ``last_consolidated: null``.
post.description: Read a markdown file from a project's system memory vault. Resolves paths under ``{data_dir}/vault/projects/<project_id>/memory/<path>``, which lives outside the regular workspace sandbox. Use this for reading consolidation markers, insight files, or knowledge entries from the agent-queue vault. Path traversal outside the project's memory directory is rejected. Returns ``{missing: true, error}`` when the file does not exist so the memory-consolidation playbook can treat it as ``last_consolidated: null``.

post.summary: Record that a file has been inspected by the codebase-inspector (or similar) workflow. Stores an entry in project memory keyed by the file path under the 'inspections' namespace, with a timestamp and optional summary. Used so future select_files_for_inspection calls can de-prioritize files that were recently inspected.
post.description: Record that a file has been inspected by the codebase-inspector (or similar) workflow. Stores an entry in project memory keyed by the file path under the 'inspections' namespace, with a timestamp and optional summary. Used so future select_files_for_inspection calls can de-prioritize files that were recently inspected.

post.summary: Search for files or content in a workspace. Use 'grep' mode to search file contents, 'find' mode to search filenames.
post.description: Search for files or content in a workspace. Use 'grep' mode to search file contents, 'find' mode to search filenames.

post.summary: Select a random sample of files from a project workspace for codebase inspection, using a weighted distribution across categories (source, specs, tests, config, recent). Automatically categorizes each tracked file by path/extension, excludes binary and generated files, and de-prioritizes files that have been inspected recently (by consulting project memory history). Returns the selected file paths plus per-category breakdown and enumeration statistics. Use this tool to implement the 'codebase-inspector' playbook's file-selection step.
post.description: Select a random sample of files from a project workspace for codebase inspection, using a weighted distribution across categories (source, specs, tests, config, recent). Automatically categorizes each tracked file by path/extension, excludes binary and generated files, and de-prioritizes files that have been inspected recently (by consulting project memory history). Returns the selected file paths plus per-category breakdown and enumeration statistics. Use this tool to implement the 'codebase-inspector' playbook's file-selection step.

post.summary: Write content to a file. Creates the file (and parent directories) if it doesn't exist, or overwrites if it does. Path can be absolute or relative to the workspaces root.
post.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file (and parent directories) if it doesn't exist, or overwrites if it does. Path can be absolute or relative to the workspaces root.

post.summary: Check out an existing branch (alias for git_checkout).
post.description: Check out an existing branch (alias for git_checkout).

post.summary: Stage all changes and commit (alias for git_commit).
post.description: Stage all changes and commit (alias for git_commit).

post.summary: Create and switch to a new git branch.
post.description: Create and switch to a new git branch.

post.summary: Create a new GitHub repository via the gh CLI.
post.description: Create a new GitHub repository via the gh CLI.

post.summary: Generate a README.md from project metadata and commit it.
post.description: Generate a README.md from project metadata and commit it.

post.summary: Get git status for all workspaces in a project. Shows branch, uncommitted changes, recent commits, ahead/behind counts, and stash count.
post.description: Get git status for all workspaces in a project. Shows branch, uncommitted changes, recent commits, ahead/behind counts, and stash count.

post.summary: List branches or create a new branch. If 'name' is provided, creates and checks out; otherwise lists branches.
post.description: List branches or create a new branch. If 'name' is provided, creates and checks out; otherwise lists branches.

post.summary: List files changed compared to a base branch.
post.description: List files changed compared to a base branch.

post.summary: Switch to an existing branch.
post.description: Switch to an existing branch.

post.summary: Stage all changes and create a commit.
post.description: Stage all changes and create a commit.

post.summary: Create a GitHub pull request using the gh CLI.
post.description: Create a GitHub pull request using the gh CLI.

post.summary: Show diff of the working tree or against a base branch.
post.description: Show diff of the working tree or against a base branch.

post.summary: Show recent commit log.
post.description: Show recent commit log.

post.summary: Merge a branch into the default branch.
post.description: Merge a branch into the default branch.

post.summary: Pull (fetch + merge) from the remote origin.
post.description: Pull (fetch + merge) from the remote origin.

post.summary: Push a branch to the remote origin.
post.description: Push a branch to the remote origin.

post.summary: Get the git remote URL (e.g. GitHub URL) for a project's workspace. Returns the origin remote URL directly from the git repository. Use this when you need the repo/GitHub URL and it's not in project metadata.
post.description: Get the git remote URL (e.g. GitHub URL) for a project's workspace. Returns the origin remote URL directly from the git repository. Use this when you need the repo/GitHub URL and it's not in project metadata.

post.summary: Merge a branch into the default branch (alias for git_merge).
post.description: Merge a branch into the default branch (alias for git_merge).

post.summary: Push the current or specified branch to origin (alias for git_push).
post.description: Push the current or specified branch to origin (alias for git_push).

post.summary: Add an MCP server registry entry by writing its vault markdown.  Watcher updates the in-memory registry and probes the new entry.
post.description: Add an MCP server registry entry by writing its vault markdown.  Watcher updates the in-memory registry and probes the new entry.

post.summary: Remove an MCP server's vault file and registry/catalog entry.  Refuses if any profile still references the name; the response lists the offending profiles.
post.description: Remove an MCP server's vault file and registry/catalog entry.  Refuses if any profile still references the name; the response lists the offending profiles.

post.summary: Update fields on an existing MCP server registry entry.  Loads the current markdown, merges the patch, writes back, and re-probes.
post.description: Update fields on an existing MCP server registry entry.  Loads the current markdown, merges the patch, writes back, and re-probes.

post.summary: Return one MCP server's full config (for editing).
post.description: Return one MCP server's full config (for editing).

post.summary: List MCP servers visible to a scope.  Omit project_id for system scope; supply it to include project-scoped servers and inherited system entries.
post.description: List MCP servers visible to a scope.  Omit project_id for system scope; supply it to include project-scoped servers and inherited system entries.

post.summary: Return the cached tool list for one or more servers.  Probed once at daemon startup; refreshed manually via probe_mcp_server.
post.description: Return the cached tool list for one or more servers.  Probed once at daemon startup; refreshed manually via probe_mcp_server.

post.summary: Re-probe a single MCP server and overwrite its tool catalog entry.  Use after editing a server config or when the server's tool list has changed externally.
post.description: Re-probe a single MCP server and overwrite its tool catalog entry.  Use after editing a server config or when the server's tool list has changed externally.

post.summary: Append content to an existing note, or create a new note if it doesn't exist. Ideal for stream-of-consciousness input -- appends with a blank line separator without needing to read and rewrite the entire note.
post.description: Append content to an existing note, or create a new note if it doesn't exist. Ideal for stream-of-consciousness input -- appends with a blank line separator without needing to read and rewrite the entire note.

post.summary: List all spec files and note files for a project side by side. Returns raw file listings (names, titles, sizes) for gap analysis. Use this when the user asks to compare specs with notes or find what's missing.
post.description: List all spec files and note files for a project side by side. Returns raw file listings (names, titles, sizes) for gap analysis. Use this when the user asks to compare specs with notes or find what's missing.

post.summary: Delete a project note by title. If the user provides the note name directly, call this tool immediately -- no need to list notes first.
post.description: Delete a project note by title. If the user provides the note name directly, call this tool immediately -- no need to list notes first.

post.summary: List all notes for a project. Returns name (filename), title, and size for each note. Use the 'name' field when calling read_note or delete_note.
post.description: List all notes for a project. Returns name (filename), title, and size for each note. Use the 'name' field when calling read_note or delete_note.

post.summary: Explicitly incorporate a note's content into the project profile. Uses an LLM to integrate the note's knowledge into the living profile rather than simply appending. Use when a note contains important knowledge that should be part of the project's core understanding.
post.description: Explicitly incorporate a note's content into the project profile. Uses an LLM to integrate the note's knowledge into the living profile rather than simply appending. Use when a note contains important knowledge that should be part of the project's core understanding.

post.summary: Read a note's full contents. Returns the markdown content, path, and size. Use the 'name' field from list_notes (e.g. 'my-note.md') as the title parameter.
post.description: Read a note's full contents. Returns the markdown content, path, and size. Use the 'name' field from list_notes (e.g. 'my-note.md') as the title parameter.

post.summary: Create or overwrite a project note. Use to create new notes or to save edits (read with read_file first, modify, then write back).
post.description: Create or overwrite a project note. Use to create new notes or to save edits (read with read_file first, modify, then write back).

post.summary: Manually trigger compilation of a playbook markdown file. Provide the full markdown content (including YAML frontmatter) or a file path. Returns the compiled playbook metadata on success, or detailed errors on failure.
post.description: Manually trigger compilation of a playbook markdown file. Provide the full markdown content (including YAML frontmatter) or a file path. Returns the compiled playbook metadata on success, or detailed errors on failure.

post.summary: Create a new playbook markdown file in the vault at the scope-appropriate location. Does NOT compile — authors iterate on the source and compile explicitly via update_playbook_source (or let the vault watcher pick it up). Fails if a playbook with the same id already exists anywhere in the vault.
post.description: Create a new playbook markdown file in the vault at the scope-appropriate location. Does NOT compile — authors iterate on the source and compile explicitly via update_playbook_source (or let the vault watcher pick it up). Fails if a playbook with the same id already exists anywhere in the vault.

post.summary: Archive a playbook's source file to vault/trash/playbooks/ and remove it from the active registry. Historical playbook_runs rows are preserved.
post.description: Archive a playbook's source file to vault/trash/playbooks/ and remove it from the active registry. Historical playbook_runs rows are preserved.

post.summary: Simulate playbook execution with a mock event, producing no side effects. Walks the graph from entry to terminal without real LLM calls, DB writes, or event emission. Returns the node trace showing the path that would be taken. Useful for testing and validating playbook design before deploying.
post.description: Simulate playbook execution with a mock event, producing no side effects. Walks the graph from entry to terminal without real LLM calls, DB writes, or event emission. Returns the node trace showing the path that would be taken. Useful for testing and validating playbook design before deploying.

post.summary: Return the raw markdown of a playbook plus its content hash. Used by the dashboard to load a playbook for editing; the hash is sent back on save for optimistic-concurrency conflict detection.
post.description: Return the raw markdown of a playbook plus its content hash. Used by the dashboard to load a playbook for editing; the hash is sent back on save for optimistic-concurrency conflict detection.

post.summary: Inspect a playbook run in detail. Returns full node trace (with per-node timing, transitions, and status), complete conversation history, token usage, and trigger event. Use list_playbook_runs first to find run IDs.
post.description: Inspect a playbook run in detail. Returns full node trace (with per-node timing, transitions, and status), complete conversation history, token usage, and trigger event. Use list_playbook_runs first to find run IDs.

post.summary: List recent playbook runs with status and path taken through the graph. Each run includes a compact node trace showing visited nodes and their outcome. Filter by playbook_id and/or status (e.g. 'paused' to find runs awaiting human review). Returns newest first.
post.description: List recent playbook runs with status and path taken through the graph. Each run includes a compact node trace showing visited nodes and their outcome. Filter by playbook_id and/or status (e.g. 'paused' to find runs awaiting human review). Returns newest first.

post.summary: List all playbooks across scopes with status, triggers, and last run info. Returns every active compiled playbook. Optionally filter by scope. When project_id is provided, project-scoped playbooks belonging to a different project are excluded; system and agent-type scoped playbooks are always included because they apply across projects.
post.description: List all playbooks across scopes with status, triggers, and last run info. Returns every active compiled playbook. Optionally filter by scope. When project_id is provided, project-scoped playbooks belonging to a different project are excluded; system and agent-type scoped playbooks are always included because they apply across projects.

post.summary: Get structured graph view data for dashboard rendering of a playbook. Returns nodes as positioned boxes (color-coded by type), transitions as labelled arrows, with optional overlays for live state (current node highlighting for running instances), run path highlighting, and per-node health metrics. Suitable for interactive visualization.
post.description: Get structured graph view data for dashboard rendering of a playbook. Returns nodes as positioned boxes (color-coded by type), transitions as labelled arrows, with optional overlays for live state (current node highlighting for running instances), run path highlighting, and per-node health metrics. Suitable for interactive visualization.

post.summary: Compute health metrics for playbook runs: tokens per node, run duration statistics, transition paths, and failure rates. Returns a comprehensive report with per-node metrics (avg duration, token usage, failure rate), run duration percentiles (p50, p95), most common paths through the graph, and failure analysis.
post.description: Compute health metrics for playbook runs: tokens per node, run duration statistics, transition paths, and failure rates. Returns a comprehensive report with per-node metrics (avg duration, token usage, failure rate), run duration percentiles (p50, p95), most common paths through the graph, and failure analysis.

post.summary: Recover an orphaned coordination workflow whose playbook run has died (crashed, failed, timed out). If the playbook was paused waiting for stage completion and all tasks are done, re-emits the missed event to resume the playbook. If the playbook run failed, emits a workflow.orphaned event for manual intervention. Tasks in the workflow continue executing independently regardless of playbook state.
post.description: Recover an orphaned coordination workflow whose playbook run has died (crashed, failed, timed out). If the playbook was paused waiting for stage completion and all tasks are done, re-emits the missed event to resume the playbook. If the playbook run failed, emits a workflow.orphaned event for manual intervention. Tasks in the workflow continue executing independently regardless of playbook state.

post.summary: Resume a paused (human-in-the-loop) playbook run. Provide your review decision or feedback as human_input — it will be injected into the conversation and the playbook will continue from where it paused.
post.description: Resume a paused (human-in-the-loop) playbook run. Provide your review decision or feedback as human_input — it will be injected into the conversation and the playbook will continue from where it paused.

post.summary: Manually trigger a playbook run. Executes the full compiled playbook graph from entry to terminal with real LLM calls, database persistence, and event emission. Use this to start a playbook on demand rather than waiting for its configured trigger (timer, event, etc.).
post.description: Manually trigger a playbook run. Executes the full compiled playbook graph from entry to terminal with real LLM calls, database persistence, and event emission. Use this to start a playbook on demand rather than waiting for its configured trigger (timer, event, etc.).

post.summary: Render a compiled playbook graph as an ASCII diagram or Mermaid flowchart syntax. Shows nodes (with type badges), edges, and transition conditions. Useful for understanding playbook structure and sharing visual documentation.
post.description: Render a compiled playbook graph as an ASCII diagram or Mermaid flowchart syntax. Shows nodes (with type badges), edges, and transition conditions. Useful for understanding playbook structure and sharing visual documentation.

post.summary: Write new playbook markdown to the vault atomically and compile synchronously. On successful compile returns the new version; on validation failure returns 'errors' with previous compiled version still live. If 'expected_source_hash' is supplied and does not match the current vault copy, returns a conflict error (vault changed underneath).
post.description: Write new playbook markdown to the vault atomically and compile synchronously. On successful compile returns the new version; on validation failure returns 'errors' with previous compiled version still live. If 'expected_source_hash' is supplied and does not match the current vault copy, returns a conflict error (vault changed underneath).

post.summary: Get or set plugin configuration.
post.description: Get or set plugin configuration.

post.summary: Disable a plugin (keeps it installed).
post.description: Disable a plugin (keeps it installed).

post.summary: Enable a disabled plugin.
post.description: Enable a disabled plugin.

post.summary: Get detailed info for a specific plugin.
post.description: Get detailed info for a specific plugin.

post.summary: Install a plugin from a git repository.
post.description: Install a plugin from a git repository.

post.summary: List installed plugins.
post.description: List installed plugins.

post.summary: List a plugin's prompt templates.
post.description: List a plugin's prompt templates.

post.summary: Reload a plugin (shutdown -> load -> initialize).
post.description: Reload a plugin (shutdown -> load -> initialize).

post.summary: Remove an installed plugin.
post.description: Remove an installed plugin.

post.summary: Re-copy all default prompts from plugin source.
post.description: Re-copy all default prompts from plugin source.

post.summary: Update an installed plugin.
post.description: Update an installed plugin.

post.summary: Add a workspace directory for a project. Source types: 'clone' (auto-clones from the project's repo_url), 'link' (link an existing directory on disk). Workspaces are project-scoped and dynamically acquired by agents when assigned tasks.
post.description: Add a workspace directory for a project. Source types: 'clone' (auto-clones from the project's repo_url), 'link' (link an existing directory on disk). Workspaces are project-scoped and dynamically acquired by agents when assigned tasks.

post.summary: Create a new project.  Optionally auto-create a dedicated Discord channel for the project.  When auto_create_channels is omitted the behaviour is determined by the per_project_channels.auto_create config flag.
post.description: Create a new project.  Optionally auto-create a dedicated Discord channel for the project.  When auto_create_channels is omitted the behaviour is determined by the per_project_channels.auto_create config flag.

post.summary: Delete a project and all associated data (tasks, repos, results, token ledger). Cannot delete if any task is IN_PROGRESS. In-memory channel caches are automatically purged. Optionally archive the project's Discord channels.
post.description: Delete a project and all associated data (tasks, repos, results, token ledger). Cannot delete if any task is IN_PROGRESS. In-memory channel caches are automatically purged. Optionally archive the project's Discord channels.

post.summary: Edit a project's properties: name, credit_weight, max_concurrent_agents, budget_limit, discord_channel_id, default_profile_id, or repo_default_branch. Use this to rename projects, adjust scheduling weight, set token budgets, link Discord channels, set a default agent profile, or change the default git branch.
post.description: Edit a project's properties: name, credit_weight, max_concurrent_agents, budget_limit, discord_channel_id, default_profile_id, or repo_default_branch. Use this to rename projects, adjust scheduling weight, set token budgets, link Discord channels, set a default agent profile, or change the default git branch.

post.summary: Scan project workspaces to find which ones have branches with merge conflicts against the default branch (main). Returns workspace IDs, conflicting branches, and file details. Use this BEFORE creating a merge-conflict resolution task so you can pass the correct preferred_workspace_id to create_task — ensuring the agent gets assigned the workspace that actually contains the conflict.
post.description: Scan project workspaces to find which ones have branches with merge conflicts against the default branch (main). Returns workspace IDs, conflicting branches, and file details. Use this BEFORE creating a merge-conflict resolution task so you can pass the correct preferred_workspace_id to create_task — ensuring the agent gets assigned the workspace that actually contains the conflict.

post.summary: Get full details for a single project, including repo/GitHub URL, workspace path, default branch, token usage, and configuration. Use this when you need project metadata like the GitHub/git repository URL, workspace location, or budget info. This is the go-to tool for answering questions about a project's URL, location, or setup.
post.description: Get full details for a single project, including repo/GitHub URL, workspace path, default branch, token usage, and configuration. Use this when you need project metadata like the GitHub/git repository URL, workspace location, or budget info. This is the go-to tool for answering questions about a project's URL, location, or setup.

post.summary: Get the Discord channel ID configured for a project.
post.description: Get the Discord channel ID configured for a project.

post.summary: Reverse lookup: given a Discord channel ID, find which project it belongs to. Returns the project ID, or null if no project is linked.
post.description: Reverse lookup: given a Discord channel ID, find which project it belongs to. Returns the project ID, or null if no project is linked.

post.summary: List all projects in the system.
post.description: List all projects in the system.

post.summary: List workspaces, optionally filtered by project. Shows lock status (which agent/task holds each workspace).
post.description: List workspaces, optionally filtered by project. Shows lock status (which agent/task holds each workspace).

post.summary: Pause a project so no new tasks are scheduled.
post.description: Pause a project so no new tasks are scheduled.

post.summary: Queue a high-priority Sync Workspaces task that orchestrates a full workspace synchronization workflow. When executed, the task will: (1) pause the project, (2) wait for all active tasks to complete, (3) launch a Claude Code agent to merge all feature branches into the default branch across all workspaces, (4) resume the project. Use this when workspaces have drifted from the default branch and feature work is stuck on feature branches that need consolidation.
post.description: Queue a high-priority Sync Workspaces task that orchestrates a full workspace synchronization workflow. When executed, the task will: (1) pause the project, (2) wait for all active tasks to complete, (3) launch a Claude Code agent to merge all feature branches into the default branch across all workspaces, (4) resume the project. Use this when workspaces have drifted from the default branch and feature work is stuck on feature branches that need consolidation.

post.summary: Release (remove) a scheduling constraint from a project. If specific fields are provided, only those fields are cleared; otherwise the entire constraint is removed.
post.description: Release (remove) a scheduling constraint from a project. If specific fields are provided, only those fields are cleared; otherwise the entire constraint is removed.

post.summary: Force-release a stuck workspace lock. Use when a workspace is locked by a dead agent or stale task.
post.description: Force-release a stuck workspace lock. Use when a workspace is locked by a dead agent or stale task.

post.summary: Delete a workspace from a project. The workspace must not be locked by an agent. Accepts a workspace ID or name. Does not delete files on disk — only removes the workspace record from the database.
post.description: Delete a workspace from a project. The workspace must not be locked by an agent. Accepts a workspace ID or name. Does not delete files on disk — only removes the workspace record from the database.

post.summary: Resume a paused project.
post.description: Resume a paused project.

post.summary: Set a project's channel by channel name (string lookup). Resolves the channel name within the guild. Deprecated — prefer edit_project with discord_channel_id.
post.description: Set a project's channel by channel name (string lookup). Resolves the channel name within the guild. Deprecated — prefer edit_project with discord_channel_id.

post.summary: Set (or change) the default git branch for a project. If the branch does not exist on the remote yet, it will be created automatically from the current default branch. Use this when a project should branch off of and merge into a branch other than 'main' (e.g. 'dev').
post.description: Set (or change) the default git branch for a project. If the branch does not exist on the remote yet, it will be created automatically from the current default branch. Use this when a project should branch off of and merge into a branch other than 'main' (e.g. 'dev').

post.summary: Link an existing Discord channel to a project. Deprecated — prefer edit_project with discord_channel_id.
post.description: Link an existing Discord channel to a project. Deprecated — prefer edit_project with discord_channel_id.

post.summary: Set a temporary scheduling constraint on a project. Supports exclusive access (only one agent at a time), per-agent-type concurrency limits, and pausing all scheduling. Constraints stack: calling this on a project that already has a constraint merges the new fields with the existing ones.
post.description: Set a temporary scheduling constraint on a project. Supports exclusive access (only one agent at a time), per-agent-type concurrency limits, and pausing all scheduling. Constraints stack: calling this on a project that already has a constraint merges the new fields with the existing ones.

post.summary: Get Claude Code usage stats from live session data. Computes real token usage by scanning active session JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/. Also reads subscription info from ~/.claude/.credentials.json.
post.description: Get Claude Code usage stats from live session data. Computes real token usage by scanning active session JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/. Also reads subscription info from ~/.claude/.credentials.json.

post.summary: Get recent system events (task completions, failures, agent questions, budget warnings, etc.) from the event database. Supports filtering by event type, time range, project, agent, or task.
post.description: Get recent system events (task completions, failures, agent questions, budget warnings, etc.) from the event database. Supports filtering by event type, time range, project, agent, or task.

post.summary: Get a high-level overview of the system: projects, agents, tasks counts.
post.description: Get a high-level overview of the system: projects, agents, tasks counts.

post.summary: Return tasks stuck in ASSIGNED or IN_PROGRESS beyond their per-status threshold.  Detection and time arithmetic run in the database — the caller passes thresholds and a reference ``now`` timestamp and receives a structured list back.  Defaults match the system-health-check playbook's stuck definition: ASSIGNED > 30 minutes, IN_PROGRESS > 2 hours.  Each entry carries ``id``, ``project_id``, ``status``, ``assigned_agent``, ``updated_at``, and ``seconds_in_state`` so remediation (``restart_task`` vs ``set_task_status(..., status="READY")``) can branch on the agent state.
post.description: Return tasks stuck in ASSIGNED or IN_PROGRESS beyond their per-status threshold.  Detection and time arithmetic run in the database — the caller passes thresholds and a reference ``now`` timestamp and receives a structured list back.  Defaults match the system-health-check playbook's stuck definition: ASSIGNED > 30 minutes, IN_PROGRESS > 2 hours.  Each entry carries ``id``, ``project_id``, ``status``, ``assigned_agent``, ``updated_at``, and ``seconds_in_state`` so remediation (``restart_task`` vs ``set_task_status(..., status="READY")``) can branch on the agent state.

post.summary: Get token usage breakdown by project or task.
post.description: Get token usage breakdown by project or task.

post.summary: List event types that are valid playbook triggers, grouped by category (e.g. 'git', 'task', 'file'). Excludes 'notify.*' transport events and dynamically-generated 'timer.*' / 'cron.*' types (UIs should offer those via a dedicated picker). Intended for trigger-picker components in the dashboard and CLI.
post.description: List event types that are valid playbook triggers, grouped by category (e.g. 'git', 'task', 'file'). Excludes 'notify.*' transport events and dynamically-generated 'timer.*' / 'cron.*' types (UIs should offer those via a dedicated picker). Intended for trigger-picker components in the dashboard and CLI.

post.summary: List all prompt templates for a project. Returns name, description, category, tags, and variable schemas for each template. Optionally filter by category (system, task, hooks, custom) or tag.
post.description: List all prompt templates for a project. Returns name, description, category, tags, and variable schemas for each template. Optionally filter by category (system, task, hooks, custom) or tag.

post.summary: Pause, resume, or check the status of the orchestrator (task scheduler). When paused, no new tasks will be assigned to agents.
post.description: Pause, resume, or check the status of the orchestrator (task scheduler). When paused, no new tasks will be assigned to agents.

post.summary: Provide a human reply to an agent question (WAITING_INPUT → READY). The agent's question is answered by appending the human's response to the task description so the agent sees it on re-execution.
post.description: Provide a human reply to an agent question (WAITING_INPUT → READY). The agent's question is answered by appending the human's response to the task description so the agent sees it on re-execution.

post.summary: Read and filter the daemon's structured JSONL log file. Returns parsed log entries with severity, timestamps, and context fields. Use this to inspect detailed system behavior, diagnose errors, or analyze component-level activity.
post.description: Read and filter the daemon's structured JSONL log file. Returns parsed log entries with severity, timestamps, and context fields. Use this to inspect detailed system behavior, diagnose errors, or analyze component-level activity.

post.summary: Read a prompt template's full content and metadata. Returns the template body, variable definitions, tags, and category. Pass either (project_id, name) for a project-scoped template, or path=<absolute path> for a bundled template that ships with the daemon (in playbooks, use aq://prompts/<path> — compiler rewrites).
post.description: Read a prompt template's full content and metadata. Returns the template body, variable definitions, tags, and category. Pass either (project_id, name) for a project-scoped template, or path=<absolute path> for a bundled template that ships with the daemon (in playbooks, use aq://prompts/<path> — compiler rewrites).

post.summary: Manually trigger a config hot-reload from disk. Returns a summary of which sections changed, which were applied, and which require a daemon restart.
post.description: Manually trigger a config hot-reload from disk. Returns a summary of which sections changed, which were applied, and which require a daemon restart.

post.summary: Render a prompt template with variable substitution. Replaces {{variable}} placeholders with provided values. Returns the fully rendered prompt text. Pass either (project_id, name) for a project-scoped template, or path=<absolute path> for a bundled template (in playbooks, use aq://prompts/<path> — compiler rewrites).
post.description: Render a prompt template with variable substitution. Replaces {{variable}} placeholders with provided values. Returns the fully rendered prompt text. Pass either (project_id, name) for a project-scoped template, or path=<absolute path> for a bundled template (in playbooks, use aq://prompts/<path> — compiler rewrites).

post.summary: Restart the agent-queue daemon process. The bot will disconnect briefly and reconnect. A reason is required. Use wait_for_tasks=true to let running tasks finish before restarting.
post.description: Restart the agent-queue daemon process. The bot will disconnect briefly and reconnect. A reason is required. Use wait_for_tasks=true to let running tasks finish before restarting.

post.summary: Execute a shell command in a workspace directory. Use when the user asks to run tests, check status, etc.
post.description: Execute a shell command in a workspace directory. Use when the user asks to run tests, check status, etc.

post.summary: Scan vault reference stubs to detect staleness.  Compares each stub's recorded source_hash against the current source file on disk.  Reports stubs that are stale (source changed), missing (source deleted), unenriched (placeholder content), or orphaned (no source metadata).  Use to audit reference stub health before triggering re-enrichment.
post.description: Scan vault reference stubs to detect staleness.  Compares each stub's recorded source_hash against the current source file on disk.  Reports stubs that are stale (source changed), missing (source deleted), unenriched (placeholder content), or orphaned (no source metadata).  Use to audit reference stub health before triggering re-enrichment.

post.summary: Shut down the bot and all running agents. Excluded from MCP by default for safety.
post.description: Shut down the bot and all running agents. Excluded from MCP by default for safety.

post.summary: Comprehensive token usage audit over a time range. Shows totals by project, top tasks, and daily breakdown.
post.description: Comprehensive token usage audit over a time range. Shows totals by project, top tasks, and daily breakdown.

post.summary: Pull the latest source from git and restart the daemon. Use wait_for_tasks=true to let running tasks finish before restarting. Excluded from MCP by default for safety.
post.description: Pull the latest source from git and restart the daemon. Use wait_for_tasks=true to let running tasks finish before restarting. Excluded from MCP by default for safety.

post.summary: Add a dependency between two tasks: task_id will depend on depends_on (i.e. task_id cannot start until depends_on is completed). Validates that both tasks exist and performs cycle detection to prevent circular dependency chains. Use this when the user wants to link tasks so one must finish before another can begin.
post.description: Add a dependency between two tasks: task_id will depend on depends_on (i.e. task_id cannot start until depends_on is completed). Validates that both tasks exist and performs cycle detection to prevent circular dependency chains. Use this when the user wants to link tasks so one must finish before another can begin.

post.summary: Approve a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status. Creates subtasks from the stored plan and marks the task completed.
post.description: Approve a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status. Creates subtasks from the stored plan and marks the task completed.

post.summary: Manually approve and complete a task that is AWAITING_APPROVAL. Use for tasks on LINK repos that don't have GitHub PRs.
post.description: Manually approve and complete a task that is AWAITING_APPROVAL. Use for tasks on LINK repos that don't have GitHub PRs.

post.summary: Return the current auto-archive configuration. Shows archive policy settings plus the count of currently archived tasks and how many terminal tasks are eligible right now.
post.description: Return the current auto-archive configuration. Shows archive policy settings plus the count of currently archived tasks and how many terminal tasks are eligible right now.

post.summary: Archive tasks. Provide task_id to archive a single task, or project_id to bulk-archive all completed tasks in a project.
post.description: Archive tasks. Provide task_id to archive a single task, or project_id to bulk-archive all completed tasks in a project.

post.summary: Create a task for an agent to execute. This is your PRIMARY tool for getting work done — prefer creating tasks over doing file work yourself. Agents have full context windows, isolated workspaces, and can run in parallel. Task descriptions must be completely self-contained with all context the agent needs. If no project_id is given, it inherits from the active project.
post.description: Create a task for an agent to execute. This is your PRIMARY tool for getting work done — prefer creating tasks over doing file work yourself. Agents have full context windows, isolated workspaces, and can run in parallel. Task descriptions must be completely self-contained with all context the agent needs. If no project_id is given, it inherits from the active project.

post.summary: Delete a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status. Completes the task without creating any subtasks.
post.description: Delete a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status. Completes the task without creating any subtasks.

post.summary: Delete a task. Cannot delete a task that is currently in progress.
post.description: Delete a task. Cannot delete a task that is currently in progress.

post.summary: Edit a task's properties: project_id, title, description, priority, task_type, status, max_retries, verification_type, profile_id, auto_approve_plan, skip_verification, agent_type, affinity_agent_id, affinity_reason, or workspace_mode. Use this to move a task to a different project, rename tasks, change priority, override status (admin), assign a profile, adjust retry/verification settings, or set coordination parameters.
post.description: Edit a task's properties: project_id, title, description, priority, task_type, status, max_retries, verification_type, profile_id, auto_approve_plan, skip_verification, agent_type, affinity_agent_id, affinity_reason, or workspace_mode. Use this to move a task to a different project, rename tasks, change priority, override status (admin), assign a profile, adjust retry/verification settings, or set coordination parameters.

post.summary: Check dependency chain health. Shows downstream tasks stuck because of blocked tasks. Pass task_id for a specific task, or project_id for all stuck chains in a project.
post.description: Check dependency chain health. Shows downstream tasks stuck because of blocked tasks. Pass task_id for a specific task, or project_id for all stuck chains in a project.

post.summary: Get full details of a specific task including its description.
post.description: Get full details of a specific task including its description.

post.summary: Get the full dependency graph for a specific task: what it depends on (upstream) and what it blocks (downstream). Each entry includes the task's id, title, and current status. Use this when the user asks why a task is blocked, what depends on a task, or wants to understand the dependency chain for a specific task. Example: 'Task X is blocked because it depends on Y which is still IN_PROGRESS.'
post.description: Get the full dependency graph for a specific task: what it depends on (upstream) and what it blocks (downstream). Each entry includes the task's id, title, and current status. Use this when the user asks why a task is blocked, what depends on a task, or wants to understand the dependency chain for a specific task. Example: 'Task X is blocked because it depends on Y which is still IN_PROGRESS.'

post.summary: Retrieve a task's output: summary, files changed, error message, tokens used. Use this when the user asks about what a task did or its results.
post.description: Retrieve a task's output: summary, files changed, error message, tokens used. Use this when the user asks about what a task did or its results.

post.summary: Get the subtask hierarchy for a specific parent task, rendered as a tree with box-drawing characters. Returns a 'display' field with pre-formatted text showing the full parent->child hierarchy, status emojis, and a progress summary. Use this when the user asks about subtasks of a specific task or wants to inspect a plan's structure. For a project-wide tree view, use list_tasks with display_mode='tree' instead.
post.description: Get the subtask hierarchy for a specific parent task, rendered as a tree with box-drawing characters. Returns a 'display' field with pre-formatted text showing the full parent->child hierarchy, status emojis, and a progress summary. Use this when the user asks about subtasks of a specific task or wants to inspect a plan's structure. For a project-wide tree view, use list_tasks with display_mode='tree' instead.

post.summary: List active tasks across ALL projects, grouped by project. Returns only non-terminal tasks (excludes COMPLETED, FAILED, BLOCKED) by default. Use this when the user wants a cross-project overview of everything that is queued, in-progress, or actionable. Response includes 'by_project' (grouped), 'tasks' (flat list), 'total', 'project_count', and 'hidden_completed' (number of terminal tasks not shown). When presenting results, say 'N active tasks across M projects'.
post.description: List active tasks across ALL projects, grouped by project. Returns only non-terminal tasks (excludes COMPLETED, FAILED, BLOCKED) by default. Use this when the user wants a cross-project overview of everything that is queued, in-progress, or actionable. Response includes 'by_project' (grouped), 'tasks' (flat list), 'total', 'project_count', and 'hidden_completed' (number of terminal tasks not shown). When presenting results, say 'N active tasks across M projects'.

post.summary: List archived tasks. Shows tasks that were previously completed and archived. Use this when the user wants to see old/finished tasks that have been cleared from the active view.
post.description: List archived tasks. Shows tasks that were previously completed and archived. Use this when the user wants to see old/finished tasks that have been cleared from the active view.

post.summary: List tasks, optionally filtered by project or status. IMPORTANT: By default, completed/failed/blocked tasks are HIDDEN and only active tasks are returned. When presenting results from the default filter, say 'N active tasks' (not just 'N tasks'). Use show_all=true or include_completed=true to include finished tasks. Use completed_only=true to see only finished tasks. An explicit status filter overrides all convenience flags. Supports three display modes: 'flat' (default list), 'tree' (hierarchical view with subtasks), and 'compact' (root tasks with progress bars). Tree and compact modes require project_id. Use show_dependencies=true to annotate each task with its upstream depends_on and downstream blocks relationships — useful for understanding blocking chains.
post.description: List tasks, optionally filtered by project or status. IMPORTANT: By default, completed/failed/blocked tasks are HIDDEN and only active tasks are returned. When presenting results from the default filter, say 'N active tasks' (not just 'N tasks'). Use show_all=true or include_completed=true to include finished tasks. Use completed_only=true to see only finished tasks. An explicit status filter overrides all convenience flags. Supports three display modes: 'flat' (default list), 'tree' (hierarchical view with subtasks), and 'compact' (root tasks with progress bars). Tree and compact modes require project_id. Use show_dependencies=true to annotate each task with its upstream depends_on and downstream blocks relationships — useful for understanding blocking chains.

post.summary: Manually scan project workspaces for plan.md files and present them for approval. Use when the supervisor missed auto-detection or a plan was dropped into a workspace manually.
post.description: Manually scan project workspaces for plan.md files and present them for approval. Use when the supervisor missed auto-detection or a plan was dropped into a workspace manually.

post.summary: Internal: Process a task completion to discover and archive plan files. Called by Supervisor after task completion.
post.description: Internal: Process a task completion to discover and archive plan files. Called by Supervisor after task completion.

post.summary: Reject a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status with feedback. Reopens the task so the agent can revise the plan.
post.description: Reject a plan for a task in AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL status with feedback. Reopens the task so the agent can revise the plan.

post.summary: Remove a dependency between two tasks: task_id will no longer depend on depends_on. Use this when the user wants to unlink tasks or remove a blocking relationship.
post.description: Remove a dependency between two tasks: task_id will no longer depend on depends_on. Use this when the user wants to unlink tasks or remove a blocking relationship.

post.summary: Reopen a completed or failed task with feedback. Use this when a task needs rework — the feedback is appended to the task description and stored as a structured context entry so the agent sees it on re-execution. The task is reset to READY, retry count is cleared, and the PR URL is removed so a fresh PR can be created.
post.description: Reopen a completed or failed task with feedback. Use this when a task needs rework — the feedback is appended to the task description and stored as a structured context entry so the agent sees it on re-execution. The task is reset to READY, retry count is cleared, and the PR URL is removed so a fresh PR can be created.

post.summary: Reset a completed, failed, or blocked task back to READY so it gets picked up by an agent again.
post.description: Reset a completed, failed, or blocked task back to READY so it gets picked up by an agent again.

post.summary: Directly set a task's status. Administrative override — use with care as it bypasses normal state machine transitions.
post.description: Directly set a task's status. Administrative override — use with care as it bypasses normal state machine transitions.

post.summary: Skip a BLOCKED or FAILED task to unblock its dependency chain. Marks the task as COMPLETED so downstream dependents can proceed.
post.description: Skip a BLOCKED or FAILED task to unblock its dependency chain. Marks the task as COMPLETED so downstream dependents can proceed.

post.summary: Stop a task that is currently in progress. Cancels the agent working on it and marks the task as BLOCKED.
post.description: Stop a task that is currently in progress. Cancels the agent working on it and marks the task as BLOCKED.

post.summary: Return upstream dependencies and downstream dependents for a task. Shows a focused dependency view with visual status for each related task.
post.description: Return upstream dependencies and downstream dependents for a task. Shows a focused dependency view with visual status for each related task.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: The task that should wait (downstream task)

depends_on.title: Depends On
depends_on.description: The task that must complete first (upstream task)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to add the workspace to

source.title: Source
source.description: How to set up the workspace: clone (from project repo_url), link (existing dir)

path.title: Path
path.description: Directory path (required for link, auto-generated for clone)

content.title: Content
content.description: Content to append (or initial content if creating)

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Task ID whose plan to approve

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Archive a single task by ID (must be COMPLETED, FAILED, or BLOCKED)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Bulk-archive all completed tasks in this project (alternative to task_id)

include_failed.title: Include Failed
include_failed.description: When bulk-archiving by project_id, also archive FAILED and BLOCKED tasks. Default false.

markdown.title: Markdown
markdown.description: Full playbook markdown content including YAML frontmatter. Frontmatter must include: id, triggers (list), scope (system|project|agent-type:xxx). One of 'markdown', 'path', or 'playbook_id' is required.

path.title: Path
path.description: Absolute path to a playbook .md file on disk. If provided, the file is read and used as the markdown.

playbook_id.title: Playbook Id
playbook_id.description: ID of an already-compiled playbook. Resolves to its source path via the playbook manager and recompiles it. Use this to recompile by ID without remembering the vault path. One of 'markdown', 'path', or 'playbook_id' is required.

force.title: Force
force.description: Force recompilation even if source is unchanged. Defaults to true for manual compilation.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID. Must contain only alphanumerics, hyphens, and underscores — no path separators or traversal segments.

path.title: Path
path.description: Relative subdirectory under the project's memory directory (e.g. ``insights`` or ``knowledge``).

newer_than.title: Newer Than
newer_than.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp. If provided, only files whose mtime is strictly newer than this time are counted. Omit or pass null to count all files.

playbook_id.title: Playbook Id
playbook_id.description: The new playbook identifier (used as filename without .md).

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Where the file lives on disk: 'system', 'project:<project_id>', or 'agent-type:<type>'. The frontmatter scope field takes the bare form ('system' / 'project' / 'agent-type:<type>') because the project id is recovered from the vault path.

markdown.title: Markdown
markdown.description: Full markdown content including YAML frontmatter.

system_prompt_suffix.title: System Prompt Suffix
system_prompt_suffix.description: Text appended to the agent's system prompt (optional)

max_concurrent_agents.title: Max Concurrent Agents
max_concurrent_agents.description: Max agents working on this project simultaneously

repo_url.title: Repo Url
repo_url.description: Git repository URL for this project (optional)

auto_create_channels.title: Auto Create Channels
auto_create_channels.description: If true, auto-create dedicated Discord channels for this project after creation.  If false, skip channel creation.  When omitted, falls back to the global per_project_channels.auto_create config setting.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (optional — inferred from active project)

description.title: Description
description.description: Complete, self-contained instructions for the agent. Include ALL context: file paths, requirements, error messages, expected behavior, relevant code snippets, and design decisions from this conversation. Write as if the agent has never seen this conversation.

requires_approval.title: Requires Approval
requires_approval.description: If true, agent work creates a PR instead of auto-merging. Human must approve/merge the PR.

task_type.title: Task Type
task_type.description: Categorize the task type for display and filtering (optional)

profile_id.title: Profile Id
profile_id.description: Agent profile ID to configure the agent with specific tools/capabilities (optional)

preferred_workspace_id.title: Preferred Workspace Id
preferred_workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to prefer when assigning this task to an agent. Use this when the task must run in a specific workspace (e.g. one that contains a merge conflict). Get the ID from find_merge_conflict_workspaces or list_workspaces.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: List of absolute file paths to images or files that the agent should have access to when working on this task. These are typically paths to Discord attachment images that were downloaded locally. The agent will be told to read these files using the Read tool.

auto_approve_plan.title: Auto Approve Plan
auto_approve_plan.description: If true, any plan this task generates will be automatically approved without waiting for human review.

skip_verification.title: Skip Verification
skip_verification.description: If true, skip git verification on task completion. Use for investigation/research tasks that don't produce code changes requiring git cleanup.

agent_type.title: Agent Type
agent_type.description: Type of agent needed for this task (e.g. 'coding', 'code-review', 'qa'). Used by coordination playbooks to match tasks with appropriately-typed agents.

affinity_agent_id.title: Affinity Agent Id
affinity_agent_id.description: Preferred agent ID for context continuity. The scheduler will prefer this agent when assigning the task, but will fall back to any available agent if the preferred one is busy.

affinity_reason.title: Affinity Reason
affinity_reason.description: Why this agent is preferred: 'context' (has relevant conversation history), 'workspace' (already has the workspace locked), 'type' (matches the required agent type).

workspace_mode.title: Workspace Mode
workspace_mode.description: Workspace lock mode. 'exclusive' (default): one agent per workspace. 'branch-isolated': multiple agents on separate branches in the same repo. 'directory-isolated': multiple agents on separate directories (future).

title.title: Title
title.description: Note filename from list_notes 'name' field (e.g. 'my-note.md'), or the note title

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Task ID whose plan to delete

archive_channels.title: Archive Channels
archive_channels.description: If true, archive the project's Discord channels (rename + set read-only) instead of leaving them as-is. Default: false.

playbook_id.title: Playbook Id
playbook_id.description: The compiled playbook ID to simulate

event.title: Event
event.description: Mock trigger event data. Defaults to {"type": "dry_run"} if not provided. Include fields your playbook expects (e.g. project_id, task_id) for realistic simulation.

path.title: Path
path.description: File path (absolute or relative to workspaces root)

old_string.title: Old String
old_string.description: Exact text to find and replace

replace_all.title: Replace All
replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences (default false -- requires unique match)

budget_limit.title: Budget Limit
budget_limit.description: Token budget limit (optional, null to clear)

discord_channel_id.title: Discord Channel Id
discord_channel_id.description: Discord channel ID to link (optional, null to unlink)

default_profile_id.title: Default Profile Id
default_profile_id.description: Default agent profile ID for tasks in this project (optional, null to clear)

repo_default_branch.title: Repo Default Branch
repo_default_branch.description: Default git branch for the project (e.g. main, dev, master)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Move task to a different project (optional)

task_type.title: Task Type
task_type.description: New task type (optional, set to null to clear)

status.title: Status
status.description: New status — admin override, bypasses state machine (optional)

profile_id.title: Profile Id
profile_id.description: Agent profile ID (optional, set to null to clear)

auto_approve_plan.title: Auto Approve Plan
auto_approve_plan.description: If true, any plan this task generates will be automatically approved without human review (optional)

skip_verification.title: Skip Verification
skip_verification.description: If true, skip git verification on task completion (optional)

agent_type.title: Agent Type
agent_type.description: Type of agent needed (e.g. 'coding', 'code-review', 'qa'). Set to null to clear (optional)

affinity_agent_id.title: Affinity Agent Id
affinity_agent_id.description: Preferred agent ID for context continuity. Set to null to clear (optional)

affinity_reason.title: Affinity Reason
affinity_reason.description: Why this agent is preferred. Set to null to clear (optional)

workspace_mode.title: Workspace Mode
workspace_mode.description: Workspace lock mode. Set to null to clear (optional)

create_gist.title: Create Gist
create_gist.description: If true, create a public GitHub gist and return the URL

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to scan workspaces for (optional if active project is set)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: (Optional) Check all blocked chains in a project

channel_id.title: Channel Id
channel_id.description: Discord channel ID to look up

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 10).

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Filter by event type. Exact match or prefix with '*' (e.g. 'task.*' matches task.started, task.completed, etc.).

since.title: Since
since.description: Only return events newer than this relative time. Accepts durations like '5m', '1h', '2d', '30s'.

assigned_threshold_seconds.title: Assigned Threshold Seconds
assigned_threshold_seconds.description: Max seconds a task may stay ASSIGNED before being flagged as stuck.  Default 1800 (30 minutes).

in_progress_threshold_seconds.title: In Progress Threshold Seconds
in_progress_threshold_seconds.description: Max seconds a task may stay IN_PROGRESS before being flagged as stuck.  Default 7200 (2 hours).

now.title: Now
now.description: Reference Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).  Playbooks should pass the trigger event's ``tick_time`` so repeated runs are deterministic.  Defaults to the server's current time.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Optional project filter.  When omitted, all projects are scanned.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Task ID to inspect dependencies for

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: The root/parent task ID whose subtree to display

compact.title: Compact
compact.description: When true, show only the root task with a subtask count and progress bar instead of the full expanded tree. Default false (full tree).

max_depth.title: Max Depth
max_depth.description: Maximum nesting depth to render (default 4). Deeper subtasks are collapsed into a summary.

name.title: Name
name.description: New branch name (optional -- omit to list)

base_branch.title: Base Branch
base_branch.description: Base branch to compare (default: project default)

base_branch.title: Base Branch
base_branch.description: Base branch to diff against (optional, defaults to working tree diff)

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: Branch to pull (optional, defaults to current)

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: Branch to push (optional, defaults to current)

path.title: Path
path.description: Directory to search in (absolute or relative to workspaces root)

path.title: Path
path.description: File or directory to search in (absolute or relative to workspaces root)

context.title: Context
context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match

output_mode.title: Output Mode
output_mode.description: Output mode: 'content' shows matching lines, 'files_with_matches' shows file paths only, 'count' shows match counts (default 'content')

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of result lines to return (default 100)

source.title: Source
source.description: YAML text or gist URL to import from

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: If true, overwrite existing profile with same ID

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: The playbook run ID to inspect

profile_id.title: Profile Id
profile_id.description: Profile ID to install deps for

include_completed.title: Include Completed
include_completed.description: When true, include completed/failed/blocked tasks too. Default false (active tasks only).

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to list agents for (optional if active project is set)

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Max number of tasks to return (default 50)

path.title: Path
path.description: Relative path within the workspace (default: root)

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.description: Workspace name or ID (default: first workspace)

playbook_id.title: Playbook Id
playbook_id.description: Filter to a specific playbook ID

status.title: Status
status.description: Filter by run status: running, paused, completed, failed, timed_out

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Restrict project-scoped playbooks to this project. System and agent-type playbooks are still returned.

category.title: Category
category.description: Filter by category: system, task, hooks, or custom (optional)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Filter by project ID (optional, but required for tree/compact modes)

status.title: Status
status.description: Filter by exact status: DEFINED, READY, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, etc. When provided, show_all, include_completed, and completed_only are ignored.

show_all.title: Show All
show_all.description: When true, return ALL tasks regardless of status (active + completed + failed + blocked). Default false (only active tasks are shown).

include_completed.title: Include Completed
include_completed.description: When true, return all tasks including completed/failed/blocked. Alias for show_all. Default false.

completed_only.title: Completed Only
completed_only.description: When true, return ONLY completed/failed/blocked tasks. Default false.

display_mode.title: Display Mode
display_mode.description: How to format the task list. 'flat' (default) returns a JSON array of task objects. 'tree' returns a hierarchical view using box-drawing characters showing parent/subtask relationships — ideal for plans with subtasks. 'compact' returns each root task with a progress bar summarizing subtask completion. When display_mode is 'tree' or 'compact', the response includes a 'display' field with pre-formatted text.

show_dependencies.title: Show Dependencies
show_dependencies.description: When true, each task in the result includes 'depends_on' (list of upstream tasks with id and status) and 'blocks' (list of downstream task IDs). Also adds a 'dependency_display' field with pre-formatted text showing dependency relationships. Use this when the user asks about task dependencies, blocking chains, or why a task is waiting. Default false.

action.title: Action
action.description: Action to perform: pause, resume, or status

show_prompts.title: Show Prompts
show_prompts.description: Include truncated prompt previews in node labels. Default: true.

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: Overlay a specific run's path on the graph. Shows which nodes were visited, timing, and token usage per node.

include_live_state.title: Include Live State
include_live_state.description: Include live state overlay for running/paused instances. Highlights the current node. Default: true.

include_metrics.title: Include Metrics
include_metrics.description: Include per-node health metrics overlay (failure rate, avg duration, token usage). Default: false.

include_history.title: Include History
include_history.description: Include run history timeline showing past runs and paths taken. Default: false.

history_limit.title: History Limit
history_limit.description: Max runs in the history timeline (default 20).

PlaybookGraphViewResponse.title: PlaybookGraphViewResponse
PlaybookGraphViewResponse.description:
Build-graph-view output — keeps the wire shape loose because the
payload is consumed wholesale by the dashboard renderer.

playbook_id.title: Playbook Id
playbook_id.description: Filter to a specific playbook ID. Omit for all playbooks.

status.title: Status
status.description: Filter by run status: running, paused, completed, failed, timed_out.

PlaybookHealthResponse.title: PlaybookHealthResponse
PlaybookHealthResponse.description: Loose shape — compute_playbook_health returns a rich dynamic dict.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to scan for plan files. Uses active project if omitted.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Optional existing task ID to attach the plan to. If omitted, a new task is created.

workspace_path.title: Workspace Path
workspace_path.description: Path to the workspace where the task was executed

title.title: Title
title.description: Note filename from list_notes 'name' field (e.g. 'my-note.md'), or the note title

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: ID of the task waiting for input

branch_name.title: Branch Name
branch_name.description: Branch to push (optional, defaults to current)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to sync (optional if active project is set)

path.title: Path
path.description: File path (absolute or relative to workspaces root)

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-based, default 1)

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Number of lines to read. If set, overrides max_lines.

level.title: Level
level.description: Minimum log severity level. Only entries at or above this level are returned.

since.title: Since
since.description: Only return log entries newer than this relative time. Accepts durations like '5m', '1h', '2d', '30s'.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (default 100).

pattern.title: Pattern
pattern.description: Substring search in the log message/event field (case-insensitive).

title.title: Title
title.description: Note filename from list_notes 'name' field (e.g. 'my-note.md'), or the note title

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID. Must contain only alphanumerics, hyphens, and underscores — no path separators or traversal segments.

path.title: Path
path.description: Relative path within the project's memory directory (e.g. ``consolidation.md`` or ``insights/foo.md``).

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (required unless path is set)

name.title: Name
name.description: Template name from list_prompts (e.g. 'plan-generation'), or the filename (e.g. 'plan-generation.md'). Required unless path is set.

path.title: Path
path.description: Absolute filesystem path to a bundled template, e.g. '/opt/agent-queue/src/prompts/consolidation_task.md'. In playbook authoring, use aq://prompts/<name>.md instead — the playbook compiler rewrites it to an absolute path. Mutually exclusive with (project_id, name).

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID under which to record the inspection.

file_path.title: File Path
file_path.description: Relative or absolute path of the inspected file.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: Optional short summary of the inspection outcome.

findings_count.title: Findings Count
findings_count.description: Optional number of findings produced by the inspection.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Task ID whose plan to reject

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.description: Feedback describing what changes are needed in the plan

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Specific constraint fields to release: 'exclusive', 'pause_scheduling', 'max_agents_by_type'. If omitted, the entire constraint is removed.

depends_on.title: Depends On
depends_on.description: The upstream task to remove as a dependency

workspace_id.title: Workspace Id
workspace_id.description: Workspace ID or name to delete

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (used for name lookup; optional if active project is set)

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (required unless path is set)

name.title: Name
name.description: Template name to render (required unless path is set)
